meta.title: Expiatorio Park | Guadalajara
meta.description: Explore Expiatorio Park in Guadalajara, Jalisco, Mexico. Discover this beautiful urban park.

intro.title: Explore Expiatorio Park
intro.description: Expiatorio Park (Parque Expiatorio) is located in the Col Americana neighborhood of Guadalajara, Jalisco, Mexico, adjacent to the magnificent Templo Expiatorio. It is a beautiful urban square that perfectly combines solemn religious faith with a strong local street life, making it an excellent place for tourists and local families to relax and enjoy authentic street food.

visitGuide.title: Visiting Tips
items: The park is open all day, daytime visits are recommended
Enjoy the green spaces and walking paths
Great for family outings and recreational activities
Explore the nearby Col Americana neighborhood

alsoKnownAs.title: Core Highlights
items: Neo-Gothic Landmark: Adjacent to Mexico's most magnificent neo-Gothic church, extremely stunning when illuminated at night.
Family Friendly: A warm and peaceful atmosphere with green spaces and walkways for citizens to relax, where children often play.
Night Food Market: In the evening, the square transforms into a famous local night food market, a mecca for tasting affordable authentic snacks.
Art District: Located in the artistic Col Americana neighborhood, surrounded by cafes and art venues.

route.title: Tour Route
route.overview: The ideal route to explore Expiatorio Park and experience the square's tranquility and vibrant life:
steps: 16:00: Arrive at the square, visit the stained glass windows inside the Templo Expiatorio, and wait for the twelve apostles statues on the clock tower to strike the hour at 18:00.

18:30: As night falls, the landscape lights on the church's outer wall light up. At this time, food stalls in the square begin to open. You can buy roasted corn or desserts and sit on the square benches to experience the local lifestyle.
19:30: Enjoy spontaneous local community activities in the square, such as open-air Salsa dancing lessons or family interactions.

20:30: After the tour, take a short walk to see the night view of the exterior of the Museum of the Arts of the University of Guadalajara (MUSA), or find a boutique cafe to rest in the surrounding Col Americana neighborhood.
supplements: Cash Preparation: Please prepare some small denomination peso cash, as food stalls and vendors in the square usually only accept cash.
Safety Tips: Although located in a good area with relatively good security, the square is crowded at night, so please keep an eye on your phone and wallet.
Weather Advice: Guadalajara's rainy season (May-October) mostly features afternoon or evening showers, please bring an umbrella.

knowledge.title: Discover the Charm of Expiatorio Park

sections.title: Historical Significance
sections.content: Expiatorio Park is a beautiful urban park in the Col Americana neighborhood of Guadalajara. The park offers green spaces and walking paths, making it an ideal place for relaxation and exercise.

sections.title: Cultural Landscape
sections.content: The park is adjacent to the magnificent Neo-Gothic Templo Expiatorio and surrounded by artistic buildings. It's an excellent place to experience Mexican urban life and street food, full of tranquility and vitality.

sections.title: Visiting Experience
sections.content: Visiting Expiatorio Park is a relaxing experience. You can walk in the green spaces, enjoy the natural scenery, feel the warm atmosphere of the park, and taste authentic food at night.

basicInfo.title: Basic Information
basicInfo.addressValue: Col Americana, Americana, 44160 Guadalajara, Jalisco, Mexico

hours.title: Hours
hours.outdoorTime: 09:00–23:00 (Specific stall hours are mostly from evening to night)
hours.summerTime: More pleasant climate, best for outdoor visits
hours.winterTime: Warm weather, afternoon rain, bring an umbrella
hours.tip: Visit in the evening for the best night market food experience

transport.title: Transportation
transport.airportDesc: Nearest airport: Guadalajara International Airport (GDL). Take a taxi or ride-hailing service to the Col Americana neighborhood.
transport.selfDriveDesc: There are some paid public parking lots nearby, but parking may be tight at night.
transport.busDesc: You can take a bus or the light rail (Mi Macro) to Juárez station and walk.
transport.intercityDesc: Very convenient to walk if staying in Col Americana or the historic center.
transport.cyclingDesc: Taxi/ride-hailing (Uber/DiDi) is the most recommended and safe transportation method.
transport.tipsDesc: Public transport or taxi are both fine; if driving, there are paid public parking lots nearby.

reviews.title: Visitor Reviews

reviews.moreReviews: View More Reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Juan Carlos
items.text: A magical place full of history and culture. The illuminated church at night is unforgettable, highly recommended for all tourists.

items.name: María Elena
items.text: Amazing experience. The architecture is magnificent, and the square is full of local street life. This is the essence of Guadalajara.

items.name: Roberto Méndez
items.text: Very interesting place to experience authentic local life. Evening visits recommended when the night market stalls open.

items.name: Ana Lucía
items.text: An important part of Guadalajara. The neo-Gothic architecture is moving, a must-visit place.

items.name: Carlos Herrera
items.text: Great relaxing experience. The square environment is very peaceful, and there are many food stalls nearby.

items.name: Sofía Vargas
items.text: Beautiful plaza, full of the charm of city life. The apostles striking the hour on the clock tower is unforgettable, definitely worth a visit.

items.name: Miguel Ángel
items.text: Interesting urban recreational venue. You can experience local family activities up close, and the street food is very authentic.

items.name: Laura Patricia
items.text: Architectural gem of Guadalajara. Templo Expiatorio is a must-see landmark, definitely worth a stroll and taking photos.

officialManagement.title: Management
officialManagement.text:
Square/Park Area: As an urban public space, it is uniformly managed by the Guadalajara City Council (Ayuntamiento de Guadalajara).

Templo Expiatorio (Church): Managed by the Archdiocese of Guadalajara (Arquidiócesis de Guadalajara).

aboutExpiatorio.title: Templo Expiatorio: An Icon of Guadalajara

sections.title: History of the Expiatorio
sections.description: The history of the Templo Expiatorio dates back to the 19th century. Under the direction of Archbishop Pedro Loza y Pardavé, the project by prominent Italian architect Adamo Boari was selected. Construction began in 1897 and was not completed until 1972, taking 75 years due to the Mexican Revolution and economic crises.

sections.title: A Neo-Gothic Masterpiece
sections.description: The temple is a magnificent example of Neo-Gothic style, built mainly with carved stone. A particularly striking feature is its German-made clock with a musical carillon; every day at 9 a.m., 12 p.m., and 6 p.m., figures of the twelve apostles emerge. It is considered a masterpiece of Neo-Gothic architecture in Mexico.

sections.title: A Religious and Gastronomic Gathering
sections.description: The Expiatorio offers more than a spiritual experience; its esplanade is a vibrant place full of entertainment and delicious food options. You can enjoy pastries, elotes, tacos, and more. A few steps away, you can also explore the Museum of the Arts (MUSA).

sections.title: Why is it called Expiatorio?
sections.description: Although its official name is the Basilica of the Blessed Sacrament, it was given the name 'Expiatorio' in 1906 for ceremonies of expiation. It is said that if you visit the Pearl of the West and do not stop by the Expiatorio, it is as if you were never here.
